prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a snig has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the snig-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are 4 objects on a table: a snig, a yib, a rast, and a emp. The snig is underneath the yib. The yib is to-the-left-of the rast. The rast is above the emp. The snig has a yellow button on top, a purple button on side, and a green button on bottom. The yib has a purple button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The rast has a purple button on top and a yellow button on front. The emp has a purple button on top and a yellow button on front. Pressing the yellow button on the snig activates it.

Perception scenario: There are 4 objects on a shelf: a jax, a zop, a jorp, and a florp. The jorp is underneath the florp. The florp is to-the-left-of the zop. The zop is above the jax. The jax has a green button on top and a green button on bottom. The zop has a green button on top and a purple button on bottom. The jorp has a yellow button on top, a purple button on side, and a green button on bottom. The florp has a purple button on top, a yellow button on side, and a green button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the snig-analog, and which button activates it?
